discovery.run_summary: Risk-on tape with semis surging (SMH +5.2%, NVDA +5.8%, TSM +6.4%) lifting QQQ +2.1%, while energy gets crushed (XLE -4.1%) on oil pulling back below $100 despite Iran tensions. Heavy earnings dispersion in mid-caps with PRIM crash (-50%) and TMDX (-23%) as standout dislocations worth investigating; semi-equipment names (VECO, ACLS, IPGP) ripping on combination of earnings and AI-capex tailwind.

notable_index_moves: SMH +5.18% — semiconductor capex narrative reaccelerating, TSM +6.36%
XLE -4.12% — oil unwind despite Iran headlines; energy E&P names cratering on earnings into weak tape
XLU -1.42% — defensive rotation out as risk-on resumes

discoveries.name: Primoris Services Corporation
discoveries.mechanism: Earnings + officer departure (5.02) + 'other events' combined — magnitude suggests guidance cut, project writedown, or CFO/CEO exit on top of miss. A 50% single-day drop in an engineering & construction name with $5.5B cap implies the market is pricing material backlog/contract impairment, not just a quarterly miss.
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: Combination of earnings + officer departure + 8.01 'other events' in same 8-K is the classic signature of a bad-news bundle (miss plus management change plus disclosure of a problem)
research_pointers: Read the 8-K exhibits — was it CEO or CFO departing? Mid-quarter departure is far more bearish than retirement
Check if backlog was revised down or if a major project (utility/renewables) was impaired
Compare to MTZ, MYRG, EME for read-through — if peers don't move, problem is company-specific
Look for any concurrent press release about contract loss or accounting issue
discoveries.what_would_falsify: If 8-K shows planned CEO retirement with successor named and earnings only modestly missed, then -50% is overdone. If it shows restatement risk or project impairment of major scale, then move is justified or even insufficient.

discoveries.name: TransMedics Group
discoveries.mechanism: Clean earnings 8-K only (2.02 + 9.01) — no officer departure or restructuring flagged. -23% on an earnings print with no other red-flag items typically means revenue miss or guide-down on growth deceleration (TMDX has historically traded at growth multiples on OCS organ transport adoption).
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: Only 2.02 + 9.01 items present — clean earnings filing, so move is purely a numbers/guide reaction
research_pointers: Check organ-volume growth rate vs prior quarters — deceleration story?
Listen to call transcript on guidance and aviation/logistics costs
TMDX has had short-seller scrutiny historically (Scorpion); check if any short report dropped concurrently
Compare to prior reset moves — is this a third major drop in ~12 months pattern?
discoveries.what_would_falsify: If guide was reaffirmed and miss was modest, -23% is overdone and likely a setup for bounce. If full-year guide cut materially or aviation segment showed structural problems, move is justified.

discoveries.name: Veeco Instruments
discoveries.mechanism: Semi-equipment beat into a tape where SMH +5.2% and TSM +6.4% are confirming AI-capex acceleration. Veeco leverages advanced packaging (laser annealing, MOCVD) — direct beneficiary of TSM/leading-edge capex commentary.
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: Clean earnings 8-K coinciding with sector-wide semi-equipment rally; multiple expansion warranted by group rerating
research_pointers: Check if guide raised on advanced packaging exposure
Read-through to ONTO, KLAC, AEHR which are similarly exposed
Watch if HBM/AI commentary featured prominently
discoveries.what_would_falsify: If guide was in-line and move is purely sympathy/short-cover, fade risk after sector cools

discoveries.name: Axcelis Technologies
discoveries.mechanism: ACLS reports AFTER hours TODAY (in ~10 hours) — this is pre-positioning into earnings inside a +5% semi-equipment tape. 5.07 8-K is just shareholder vote results, not catalyst. The 22% pre-earnings rip is mostly sympathy with VECO/SMH and short squeeze.
discoveries.catalyst: Sector sympathy + pre-earnings positioning (reports today after close)
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: Only 5.07 (vote results) — no earnings yet; upcoming_earnings flag confirms today's after-hours print
research_pointers: ACLS is power/SiC/memory ion-implant exposed — different mix than VECO; verify if AI-capex narrative actually applies
Power semi end-market (auto, industrial) has been weak — set up for guide-down risk vs the 22% squeeze
Watch implied move from options going into print
Asymmetric risk: 22% pre-print rally creates large fade potential on any guide miss
discoveries.what_would_falsify: Strong beat-and-raise tonight justifies the move; in-line print likely fades 5-10% as the sympathy premium unwinds

discoveries.name: Kyndryl Holdings
discoveries.mechanism: 8-K has 2.05 (restructuring/exit costs) alongside earnings — explicit restructuring charge announcement. -11% reasonable for restructuring + likely soft guide in IT services with EU cloud-restriction headlines (per news) creating overhang on US IT services exposure to European clients.
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: Item 2.05 (costs associated with exit/disposal) confirms restructuring announcement, typically bearish for a turnaround story like Kyndryl
research_pointers: Quantify the restructuring charge size and what business is being exited
Check signings/book-to-bill for the quarter — Kyndryl's bull case rests on signings momentum
EU cloud sovereignty news could be tailwind (Kyndryl is non-hyperscaler) or headwind — investigate
Free cash flow guide is the key metric for KD bulls
discoveries.what_would_falsify: If restructuring is targeted at low-margin contracts and signings/FCF guide raised, -11% is overdone

discoveries.name: Comstock Resources
discoveries.mechanism: Earnings drop compounded by sector wipeout (XLE -4.1%) on oil pullback. CRK is a Haynesville natgas pure-play, so oil tape isn't the direct driver — earnings/guide must have disappointed on capex or production. Sector tape is amplifying.
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: Clean earnings 8-K into a -4% energy sector day — combination of company-specific miss and sympathy
research_pointers: Check if 2026 production guide or capex was revised
Natgas strip vs CRK breakeven — Haynesville economics highly sensitive
Read-through to EQT, AR, RRC for natgas pure-plays — if they're flat, CRK problem is idiosyncratic
discoveries.what_would_falsify: If production beat and natgas peers also down 5%+, sympathy effect explains most of move

discoveries.name: Clean Harbors
discoveries.mechanism: Clean earnings 8-K only. -8% with 4x volume on a +2.6% XLI day is meaningful underperformance. Likely guide miss on environmental services or PFAS-related cost commentary. Worth investigating because CLH typically trades stable.
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: Single 2.02 earnings filing; magnitude unusual for typically defensive name suggests notable guide concern
research_pointers: Check Safety-Kleen segment vs Environmental Services segment trends
Any commentary on PFAS opportunity timing slipping?
Read across to Republic Services, Waste Management for waste sector reads
discoveries.what_would_falsify: If guide was reaffirmed and miss small, -8% is overdone for this quality compounder

discoveries.name: GEO Group
discoveries.mechanism: Private prison/detention operator earnings beat in current administration's immigration-enforcement environment — operating leverage on detention bed contracts is the bull thesis. +21% suggests material guide raise on ICE contract expansion.
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: Clean 2.02 earnings filing in a thematically supportive policy environment
research_pointers: Check ICE contract awards and bed utilization in the release
CXW (CoreCivic) read-through — should also be bid
Debt paydown progress is the secondary thesis for GEO equity rerating
discoveries.what_would_falsify: Beat driven by one-time items rather than contract expansion would mean rally fades

catalyst_chains.primary_event: Semiconductor capex acceleration confirmed by TSM +6.4%, SMH +5.2%, NVDA +5.8% — semi-equipment names ripping

secondary_opportunities.relationship: competitor/peer to VECO in advanced packaging metrology

catalyst_chains.primary_event: GEO Group +21% on detention contract strength
Energy sector -4% on oil pullback despite Iran headlines — E&P names like CRC, CRK reporting into hostile tape
PRIM -50% — possible read-through to engineering & construction peers if problem is industry-wide (utility capex slippage, renewables project economics)

discovery.no_signals_note: No Trump posts in feed. Energy/oil weakness despite ongoing Iran-related news suggests the geopolitical risk premium is unwinding, which is its own signal — worth Michael cross-checking against spot crude action.

ai_analysis.no_signals_note: Today's AI news flow is mostly commentary/macro (Milken panel, Diller on Altman, Musk-OpenAI history, DeepSeek funding round, Samsung $1T on AI chips, SpaceX Terafab proposal, Snap-Perplexity deal ending, Google adding Reddit citations, Match Group AI cost commentary, Apple Siri settlement). None of these are concrete product launches that map cleanly onto the moving stocks in the market_data set. The big movers (PRIM -50%, TMDX -23%, VECO +25%, VCYT +25%, ACLS +22%, GEO +21%, TGTX +16%, KMT +15%, VSEC +17%, CC -15%, LTH +14%, LIVN +15%, CRK -14%, ELAN +14%, ANDE -14%, KAI +13%, CRC -12%, CLH -8%, KD -11%, IPGP +12%) all have same-day or prior-day 8-K earnings filings, indicating these moves are earnings-driven, not AI-news-driven. No credible AI-announcement-to-stock-move linkage to flag. Quiet night on the AI-reaction front; the tape is dominated by Q1 earnings season. Possible secondary thread worth monitoring separately (not flagged here as AI-selloff opportunities): KD -10.75% with a 2.05 restructuring 8-K could relate to AI-driven IT services pressure narrative, but the proximate cause is the earnings/restructuring filing, not an AI announcement in this news set.
